title: Innovative Intense Tunisian Morbier Tagine
description: A funky and bouncy Tunisian dish featuring morbier cheese and ferment kimchi, perfect for crowd pleasing, macrobiotic friendly meals. This dish combines the flavors of North Africa with the spice of Korea.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the sliced morbier cheese and cook until it is melted and lightly browned.
Add the chopped ferment kimchi and cook until it is heated through.
Add the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened.
Season the tagine with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
